purpose: Human skill spread on the FEAT-31 testing-lab tracks. These are vehicle-capability measurements (independent of PAR_REF) used to bound the k factor in PAR_REF = k x measured (FEAT-30). Two drivers at opposite ends of the skill range.

reference_headless.note: Steady-state envelope, mu 0.51-0.66 across radii 8-103 m, mean 0.577. Lower bound on cornering: the settled-trim test rejects the ragged, throttle-steered line a human corners on.

sessions.skill: expert — self-described pro-gamer level at racing games

results.why: POWER limited, not grip limited — full throttle the whole lap, the truck never reaches the friction limit at this radius. Excluded from any mu fit.

results.note: ROLLING start — the 0-100 split is flattered

sessions.skill: novice — lower-skill end of the range

observations: Arithmetic checks out on every lap: v = 2*pi*R/t and mu = v^2/(g*R) reproduce all reported figures to 3 dp.

The DRAG strip is nearly skill-blind: 16.03 s (expert) vs 16.60 s (novice), 3.5% apart, and both within 4% of the headless 16.98 s. It measures the truck, not the driver — so it is a poor discriminator for calibration but a good sanity check that the lab and the harness agree.

The SKIDPADS are where skill lives, and the spread is enormous but NOT uniform: at R=25 m the two drivers are nearly identical (mu 0.743 vs 0.708, 5% apart), while at R=60 m they differ by 3.3x (0.724 vs 0.220) and at R=150 m by 2.9x (0.647 vs 0.225).

DATA-QUALITY CAVEAT on the novice's large pads: they lapped the 25 m pad FASTER in absolute speed (47.5 km/h) than the 60 m pad (41.0 km/h). A larger radius permits more speed, so the 60 m and 150 m laps are almost certainly not limit laps at all — they look like comfortable cruising rather than a measured maximum. Treat 0.220 / 0.225 as a floor on effort, not a measurement of the driver's grip ceiling.

Consequence for FEAT-30: par cannot be fitted to a single k. The tight-radius end is skill-insensitive (both drivers near the truck's limit), while the medium/large radii span at least 3x. Whatever par targets, it will feel very different at the two ends of this range.
